contents.value: **@import** — SCSS keyword.
**@use** — SCSS keyword.
**@forward** — SCSS keyword.
**@mixin** — SCSS keyword.
**@include** — SCSS keyword.
**lighten** — Built-in function/command in SCSS.
**darken** — Built-in function/command in SCSS.
**saturate** — Built-in function/command in SCSS.
**desaturate** — Built-in function/command in SCSS.
**adjust-hue** — Built-in function/command in SCSS.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

**Flexbox** is a CSS layout model for distributing space along a single axis.
```css
display: flex; justify-content: center;
```

**CSS Grid** is a two-dimensional layout system.
```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr;
```

A **pseudo-class** selects elements in a specific state.
```css
a:hover, input:focus, li:nth-child(2)
```

CSS **custom properties** (variables) store reusable values.
```css
:root { --color: blue; } .el { color: var(--color); }
```
**Specificity** determines which CSS rule applies when multiple rules target the same element. Inline > ID > Class > Tag.

```scss
@extend selector | @extend selector !optional
```
Shares CSS rules between selectors by making the current selector inherit all styles of the extended selector, generating combined selector groups in the compiled CSS output.

```scss
map-get($map, $key) -> value | null
```
Retrieves the value associated with a key in a Sass map data structure, returning null if the key is not found, essential for theme configuration and design token lookups.

```scss
color.adjust($color, $red: null, $green: null, $blue: null, $hue: null, $saturation: null, $lightness: null, $alpha: null) -> Color
```
Creates a new color by adjusting one or more properties of an existing color by fixed amounts, providing precise control over color channel manipulation in the modern module system.

```scss
map.merge($map1, $map2) -> Map
```
Combines two maps into a new map where keys from the second map override matching keys in the first, enabling configuration override patterns and theme extension.

```scss
list.append($list, $val, $separator: auto) -> List
```
Returns a new list with the given value appended to the end, using the specified separator or inheriting the existing list separator for consistent formatting.

```scss
math.div($number1, $number2) -> Number
```
Performs division of two numbers, replacing the deprecated slash-as-division syntax with an explicit function call to avoid ambiguity with CSS separator slashes.

```scss
string.unquote($string) -> String
```
Removes quotes from a Sass string, returning it as an unquoted string suitable for use as CSS identifiers, property names, and other contexts requiring unquoted values.

```scss
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```scss
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```scss
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```scss
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```scss
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```scss
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```scss
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.
